content: Just as high school language arts curricula teach structured written reflection as a skill to improve critical writing rather than treating it as an innate ability, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by integrating scaffolded, literacy-style reflection training instead of only relying on ad-hoc in-context self-critique prompts. This approach would leverage graded reflection exercises—from identifying basic logical inconsistencies to holistic output evaluation—modeled on how reflection is taught to human learners to boost LLM self-correction performance.
